title: Workforms: Mapping questions to columns on my board
excerpt: I’m fairly new to Monday.com and have taken over for someone no longer with the company. I’ve looked through everything online and cannot find information on how questions on a form get mapped to columns on a board. Any …

posts.author: pgraunke (Pam Graunke)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m fairly new to Monday.com and have taken over for someone no longer with the company. I’ve looked through everything online and cannot find information on how questions on a form get mapped to columns on a board. Any help would be appreciated!

posts.author: Megan345 (Megan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Pam,

Basically one board column = one form question. You can create columns directly on the board and these will be added to the form, or you can create questions on the form and these will be added to the board as a column.

You can check the mapping of existing questions on the form by hovering the mouse over this symbol:

I hope that helps!

posts.author: jack456jokky (Jack Jokky)
posts.cookedHtml:
Pam Graunke:

have taken over for someone no longer with the company Ford Benefits . I’ve looked through everything online and cannot find information on how questions on a form get mapped to columns on a board. Any help would be appreciated!

Hello,

If possible, submit a test entry through the form and then check the board to see where the data appears. This can help you deduce the mapping based on the results.
